mission.text: For years, NORML France has been innovating and creating original informational tools. Public health information is not a business. Help us stay one step ahead.

final_cta.subtitle: Support the original creators. With your membership, let's regain the advantage for public health and free information.

urgencePage.heroSubtitle: Don't stand alone against the State.
urgencePage.heroDesc:
The Ministry of the Interior's report is out: repression is exploding.
NORML France opens this support unit to defend you.
urgencePage.didYouKnowText: Did you know? The SSMSI report published this December 2025 reveals that 290,000 people received a Criminal Fixed Penalty (AFD) this year. It's a historical record. Faced with this industrialized sanctioning, we have decided to make our legal tools available to everyone, for free.
urgencePage.introText: You just received this green paper: a €200 Criminal Fixed Penalty (AFD). The State relies on your silence and isolation to validate its statistics.
urgencePage.alertText: Paying the fine is an admission of the offense. It automatically leads to:
alertPoints: Funding a policy we are fighting against.
urgencePage.solutionDesc: At NORML France, we have mobilized our legal experts to create the document the State will never give you.
urgencePage.kitContentDesc: This guide, updated following the latest ministry data, contains:
kitPoints: How to avoid registration in the B2 Criminal Record
Standard letter template drafted by lawyers
Your recourses in case of a positive saliva test
urgencePage.formDisclaimer: 100% Confidential. We are activists, not merchants.
urgencePage.expertsTitle: An expert team by your side
urgencePage.expertsDesc: NORML France relies on the expertise of renowned criminal lawyers who secure decisive victories against the administration.

experts.name: Me Nicolas Hachet
experts.bio: Specialist in traffic and narcotics law. He just won a major appeal victory a few weeks ago, setting a favorable precedent for users.

experts.name: Me Emilie Boyer
experts.bio: A long-time activist, she defends users daily against procedural abuses and advocates for a fair application of the law.

experts.name: Me Xavier Pizarro
experts.bio: The illustrious lawyer from the Kanavape case before the EU Court of Justice. A leading figure at the bar, he relentlessly fights prohibition at the highest level.

faqs.q: Does the €200 fixed penalty appear on my criminal record?
faqs.a:
YES. That is the main trap. Paying the Criminal Fixed Penalty (AFD) equates to an admission of guilt. The offense is then recorded in Bulletin N°2 of the Criminal Record.

This can prevent you from accessing certain jobs (civil service, transport, security, airports, childcare) and complicate administrative procedures. Our guide explains how to request non-registration.

faqs.q: What is the deadline to pay or contest the fine?
faqs.a:
Watch the dates! You have:
- 15 days to pay the reduced amount (€150).
- 30 days if you pay online/by phone.
- 45 days to pay the standard amount (€200) or to contest.

After this deadline, the fine increases to €450. Don't let the envelope sit around.

faqs.q: How do I contest a fine for drug use?
faqs.a:
Contesting must be done via the ANTAI website or by registered mail. But beware: you cannot contest just "because you disagree".

You must raise valid legal arguments (procedural flaws, irregular checks, failure to notify rights). This is why our lawyers (Me Hachet, Me Pizarro) drafted the letter templates included in our Survival Kit.

faqs.q: Must I pay the fine before contesting (Deposit/Consignation)?
faqs.a:
This is often required. To contest, the State usually demands a "deposit" (consignation) of €150 or €200. This amount is not cashed as a payment; it is blocked during the procedure.

If you win (or if the public prosecutor drops the case), this money is refunded to you. If you simply pay the fine directly without specifying it's a deposit, the case is closed and you are guilty.

urgencePage.joinDesc:
The State took €200 from you. Don't give them your silence too.
Join the only organization fighting to make this fine the last one.
urgencePage.joinBtn: TURN MY FINE INTO A MEMBERSHIP →

disclaimer.title: Sources & Methodology
disclaimer.repression: The repression figures (€1.1bn) are from the Kopp report (OFDT) and MILDECA.
disclaimer.taxes: The potential tax revenues (€2.5bn) come from the Council of Economic Analysis (CAE) 2019 report.
disclaimer.deaths: The traffic-related deaths include only homicides linked to narco-banditry (approx. 120/year according to the Ministry of Interior).
disclaimer.arrests: The arrests (175,000/year) correspond to arrests for simple drug use in France.
